summary: ai-sdk-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer identity to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on npm install and harvests the developer's source-control identity and machine fingerprint, then POSTs the payload to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. The script reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (the GitHub CLI auth config that stores the user's oauth_token alongside user/email) to extract the SCM email and GitHub login, and combines that with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), CI provider detection, and environment variables such as GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL and EMAIL. The README claims telemetry is limited to anonymous platform/Node/architecture diagnostics; the actual payload is a targeted reconnaissance dossier on the installing developer. The destination domain is unrelated to any legitimate publisher infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
